exercises.title: Parse CSV Data with csv.reader
exercises.description: Simulate CSV parsing using csv.reader on in-memory string data. Parse a CSV string of student records, compute the average GPA, and find the top student.
exercises.starterCode:
import csv
import io

csv_data = """name,age,gpa
Alice,20,3.9
Bob,22,3.5
Carol,21,3.8
Dave,23,3.2
Eve,20,3.7"""

# TODO: Create a csv.reader from io.StringIO(csv_data)
# Use next(reader) to skip the header row

# TODO: Parse all rows and store as list of dicts:
# {"name": name, "age": int(age), "gpa": float(gpa)}

# TODO: Print total students
# Format: "Students: 5"

# TODO: Print the student with the highest GPA
# Format: "Top student: Alice (GPA: 3.90)"

# TODO: Print average GPA
# Format: "Average GPA: 3.62"
exercises.expectedOutput:
Students: 5
Top student: Alice (GPA: 3.90)
Average GPA: 3.62
hints: students = [{'name': row[0], 'age': int(row[1]), 'gpa': float(row[2])} for row in reader]
avg = sum(s['gpa'] for s in students) / len(students)

exercises.title: Parse with csv.DictReader and Aggregate
exercises.description: Use csv.DictReader to parse a sales CSV. Group total revenue by category and print a sorted summary.
exercises.starterCode:
import csv
import io

sales_csv = """product,category,price,quantity
Laptop,Electronics,999.99,3
Mouse,Electronics,29.99,10
Desk,Furniture,249.99,2
Chair,Furniture,199.99,4
Keyboard,Electronics,79.99,7
Lamp,Furniture,49.99,5"""

# TODO: Use csv.DictReader(io.StringIO(sales_csv)) to read all rows

# TODO: Compute revenue per category
# revenue = price * quantity for each row
# Group by category using a dict

# TODO: Print category totals sorted by category name
# Format: "Electronics: $3859.90"

# TODO: Print total revenue across all categories
# Format: "Total revenue: $5059.78"

exercises.title: Write CSV with csv.writer and DictWriter
exercises.description: Take a list of dicts and write it as CSV to an in-memory buffer. Then read it back and verify the data. Also demonstrate filtering rows when writing.
exercises.starterCode:
import csv
import io

employees = [
    {"name": "Alice", "department": "Engineering", "salary": 95000},
    {"name": "Bob", "department": "Marketing", "salary": 72000},
    {"name": "Carol", "department": "Engineering", "salary": 88000},
    {"name": "Dave", "department": "HR", "salary": 65000},
    {"name": "Eve", "department": "Engineering", "salary": 102000},
]

# TODO: Write all employees to a buffer using csv.DictWriter
# fieldnames = ['name', 'department', 'salary']
# Include the header row

# TODO: Read back from the buffer using csv.DictReader
# Print each employee: "Alice | Engineering | $95000"

print()

# TODO: Write ONLY Engineering employees to a second buffer
# Then print: "Engineering employees: 3"
exercises.expectedOutput:
Alice | Engineering | $95000
Bob | Marketing | $72000
Carol | Engineering | $88000
Dave | HR | $65000
Eve | Engineering | $102000

Engineering employees: 3
hints: buf.seek(0)  # rewind to start before reading back

warmup.title: Quick Recall: CSV Module Basics
warmup.description: What does this code print? Trace the csv.reader parsing of a multi-line string.
warmup.starterCode:
import csv
import io

data = "a,b,c\n1,2,3\n4,5,6"
reader = csv.reader(io.StringIO(data))
for row in reader:
    print(row)
